scope: S4096 DeepSeek-V4 sparse-MLA backward HIP/CK cooperative producer-consumer gate
why_this_branch: Late dKV-only TileLang and CK slices can be fast only when P/scaled-dP are already materialized.
Route-owned fused TileLang makes final dKV write tiny, but loses query-major and D-parallel reuse, so dQ/recompute dominates.
The useful design is query-major score/prob/dP production with a tiny score page consumed immediately by D-parallel consumers.

latency_gates_ms.read: Because the materialized dQ+dKV floor is already about 7.97 ms, a source-boundary producer can spend only about 3.16 ms before missing the current 11.13 ms sparse-attention backward boundary.

derived_gaps.read: Grouped-D proved score reuse, but 5.77 ms recompute-only is still 1.82x over the producer budget and its 15.73 ms full path loses D parallelism. The CK one-token/H16 probe is a compile surface only, not a speed surface.

active_kernel_contract.name: source_boundary_query_major_tiny_score_page_dparallel_v1
outputs: grad_q[B,S,H,D] FP32 or BF16-accumulated output contract

producer_decomposition.owner: query-major token tile plus selected window
producer_decomposition.score_page: P and scaled-dP for a tiny token/head/selected page, never full [S,topk,H]
producer_decomposition.full_runtime_gate: source tensors in, dQ+dKV out at S4096 < 11.13 ms

consumer_decomposition.requirement: D blocks remain parallel consumers of the same score page
consumer_decomposition.why: Serializing D consumers inside one CTA is what made grouped-D full latency 15.73 ms.
candidate_surfaces: single persistent HIP kernel with producer waves and D-consumer wave groups if LDS/register footprint fits
bounded tiny L2/HBM ring-buffer score page with immediate same-stream consumers if cross-CTA sharing is needed
CK block-attention style primitive only if it supports sparse route gather and custom group-aware dKV epilogue

dKV_epilogue.order: add only after producer-only gate passes
dKV_epilogue.target: tile-local segmented/group-aware reduction before global dKV writes
dKV_epilogue.must_not_use: leader-scan or dense active-group table that serializes the route-owned schedule

resource_model_read.mori_shared_memory: Not an attention score-page mechanism. MORI/SHMEM can help EP/MoE communication buffers and overlap, but it cannot make LDS-like score pages visible between sibling CTAs inside one sparse-attention kernel.

closed_non_goals: detached P/dP slab producer then dKV consumer
plain H16/H32 head split without on-chip cross-head merge
MORI shared-memory workaround for attention score pages
next_commands: Run S4096 producer-only on one MI350; reject unless median <= 3.16 ms.
Only then wire dQ+dKV output and run full source-boundary timing against 11.13 ms.
Only if full source-boundary improves, run 4x/8x TorchTitan TFLOPs materialization.

promotion_rule.single_gpu_latency: No sparse-attention promotion until S4096 source-boundary dQ+dKV is < 11.13 ms.
promotion_rule.e2e_tflops: No new 4x/8x TFLOPs claim for this path until the single-GPU boundary improves.
